member.name: Andrew Rosindell

addresses: Margaret Thatcher House, 85 Western Road, Romford, RM1 3LS

member.synopsis: Andrew Rosindell is the Reform UK MP for Romford , and has been an MP continually since 7 June 2001.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Libya: Armed Forces
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, whether he is taking steps to support the provision of British military training and the export of appropriate defence equipment to Libya to strengthen its capacity to police its borders with Chad, Sudan and Nige
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defence Engagement with Libya is focused on defence capacity building. This supports UK objectives across a range of priorities, including those related to border security and irregular migration.

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Transport: Written Questions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, when she plans to answer Questions UIN 6334 and UIN 6335, tabled by the Hon. Member for Romford on 3 June 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I can confirm the Questions UIN 6334 and UIN 6335 were answered on 1 July 2026.

writtenQuestions.heading: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office: British Indian Ocean Territory
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, how many officials from her Department with responsibility for the British Indian Ocean Territory are working a) within the British Indian Ocean Territory, b) from her Depart
writtenQuestions.answerText: The British Indian Ocean Territory is constitutionally distinct and separate from the UK. It is administered by the British Indian Ocean Territory Administration (BIOTA). The Commissioner is based in the UK and is supported by around 40 staff split betwee...

writtenQuestions.heading: British Indian Ocean Territory: Sharks
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what steps her Department is taking to protect the British Indian Ocean Territory Marine Protected Area from illegal shark poaching.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The British Indian Ocean Territory is constitutionally distinct and separate from the UK, with its own laws and Administration. It is administered by the British Indian Ocean Territory Administration (BIOTA) who have responsibility for the protection of t...

writtenQuestions.heading: British Indian Ocean Territory: Plastics
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, with reference to the UK Small Island Developing States strategy 2026 to 2030, published on13 January 2026, what steps she is taking to protect the British Overseas Territori
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK is committed to strengthening the economic resilience of the Overseas Territories (OTs), including the resilience of critical infrastructure.We support the OTs to diversify their economies, strengthen their fiscal governance, and invest in the upgr...

writtenQuestions.heading: British Indian Ocean Territory: Economic Situation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, with reference to the UK Small Island Developing States strategy 2026 to 2030, published 13 January 2026, what steps she is taking to protect the British Overseas Territories
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK is committed to strengthening the economic resilience of the Overseas Territories (OTs), including the resilience of critical infrastructure.We support the OTs to diversify their economies, strengthen their fiscal governance, and invest in the upgr...

writtenQuestions.heading: British Indian Ocean Territory: Infrastructure
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what assessment she has made of the resilience of critical infrastructure in the British Overseas Territories.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK is committed to strengthening the economic resilience of the Overseas Territories (OTs), including the resilience of critical infrastructure.We support the OTs to diversify their economies, strengthen their fiscal governance, and invest in the upgr...

writtenQuestions.heading: Chagos Islands: Sovereignty
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, whether she has made an assessment of the potential merits of the resettlement of the British Indian Ocean Territory.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Treaty which the UK signed with Mauritius in May 2025 provides a clear path to resettlement on the Archipelago. Under the terms of the Treaty, the Government of Mauritius will be able to start a programme of resettlement on islands other than Diego Ga...

writtenQuestions.heading: Type 83 Destroyers: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, if he will make it his policy to fund the Type 83 Destroyer programme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Investment Plan sets out the Royal Navy’s (RN) approach to delivering the UK’s most advanced maritime air defence capability and the rationale for this approach. Rather than delivering this capability through a small number of large and expens...

writtenQuestions.heading: Fraud: Victims
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what recent steps he has taken to support victims of fraud.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to supporting all victims of crime, including victims of fraud.The Report Fraud Victim Service offers consistent specialist support for victims across England and Wales and covers all 43 police forces. The service aims to help...

writtenQuestions.heading: Domestic Abuse: Victim Support Schemes
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what recent steps he has taken to support victims of domestic abuse.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 18 December 2025, we published our VAWG Strategy; ‘Freedom from violence and abuse: a cross-government strategy to build a safer society for women and girls’, which sets out how we will support all victims of VAWG, including domestic abuse.Recently, th...

writtenQuestions.heading: Seabed
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he has taken to enhance the seabed warfare capabilities of the Royal Navy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Royal Navy (RN) continues to enhance its seabed warfare capabilities through a combination of investment in new technologies, platforms, and international partnerships.This includes the development of advanced autonomous and remotely operated systems ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Unmanned Air Systems: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, What steps he has taken to procure counter-unmanned aerial systems (C-UAS) for HM Armed Forces.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department continually considers and develops improvements to Integrated Air and Missile Defence systems, with investments prioritised appropriately against the assessed threat picture as part of the future Integrated Force. As set out in the Defence ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Unmanned Air Systems: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he has taken to procure long-range strike drones.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Investment Plan (DIP) sets out the Government’s commitment to invest more than £5 billion in autonomous systems over the next four years, including drones and other uncrewed capabilities, with at least £210 million on low-cost, long-range one-...

writtenQuestions.heading: Envoy IV Aircraft: Repairs and Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, if he will make it his department’s policy to upgrade the fleet of RAF Envoy IV jets.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer I gave to the hon. Member for Huntingdon (Ben Obese-Jecty) to Question 9871 on 24 June 2026: The work to enable Envoy IV aircraft operations in non-benign airspace has been paused, and there is currently no definitive...

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisons: Construction
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, if he will make it his Department’s policy to build more prisons.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in the December 2024 10-Year Prison Capacity Strategy, we are committed to delivering an additional 14,000 prison places across England and Wales and have already delivered around 3,200 places since July 2024. We remain on track to meet this co...

writtenQuestions.heading: Prison Accommodation: Construction
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what recent steps he has taken to expand the capacity of English prisons.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in the December 2024 10-Year Prison Capacity Strategy, we are committed to delivering an additional 14,000 prison places across England and Wales and have already delivered around 3,200 places since July 2024. We remain on track to meet this co...

writtenQuestions.heading: Small Businesses: Local Government
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what recent steps he has taken to enable local authorities to better support small business leaders.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is equipping local authorities with the tools to help businesses succeed. Through our devolution agenda, we are empowering local authorities and Mayoral Strategic Authorities with greater responsibility for driving local economic growth. Lo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Business: Fraud
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps he has taken to support local authorities in investigating fraudulent businesses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is committed to taking action against illicit activity on high streets. I refer the hon. Member to the Written Ministerial Statement (⁠HCWS32) made on 19 May 2026. I also refer the hon. Member to the Westminster Hall Debate on High Street S...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Markets: Local Government
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what recent steps he has taken to support local authorities in supporting market traders.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government recognises that markets can make a significant contribution to the vibrancy and diversity of our high streets and town centres. It is for local authorities, not central government, to make decisions on running, supporting and investing in l...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
